react.question: Which is the most popular JavaScript framework?
Which company invented React?
What's the fundamental building block of React apps?
What's the name of the syntax we use to describe the UI in React components?
How does data flow naturally in React apps?
How to pass data into a child component?
When to use derived state?
options: Whenever the state should not trigger a re-render
Whenever the state can be synchronized with an effect
Whenever the state should be accessible to all components
Whenever the state can be computed from another state variable

react.question: What triggers a UI re-render in React?
options: Adding event listeners to DOM elements

react.question: When do we directly "touch" the DOM in React?
options: When we need to listen to an event
When we need to change the UI
When we need to add styles

react.question: In what situation do we use a callback to update state?
options: When updating the state will be slow
When the updated state is very data-intensive
When the state update should happen faster
When the new state depends on the previous state

react.question: If we pass a function to useState, when will that function be called?
options: Each time we update the state
The first time we update the state

react.question: Which hook to use for an API request on the component's initial render?
Which variables should go into the useEffect dependency array?
options: All state and props referenced in the effect
All variables needed for clean up

react.question: An effect will always run on the initial render.
options: It depends on the dependency array
In depends on the code in the effect

react.question: When will an effect run if it doesn't have a dependency array?
options: The first time the component re-renders
Each time the component is re-rendered

react.question: What is the purpose of the 'key' prop in React lists?
options: To specify the order of elements in the list
To help React identify which items have changed, are added, or are removed
To define the styling for each element in the list
To provide a unique identifier for each element in the list

react.question: What is the key difference between React.Component and React.PureComponent?
options: React.Component supports lifecycle methods, while React.PureComponent does not
React.PureComponent does shallow comparison of props and state before re-rendering
React.Component is deprecated in favor of React.PureComponent
React.PureComponent is used only for class components, while React.Component is used for functional components

react.question: What is the significance of the 'children' prop in React components?
options: It defines the styling for child components
It passes data from parent to child components
It represents the content between the opening and closing tags of the component
It triggers a re-render of the component when its value changes

react.question: What is the purpose of React Fragments?
options: To group multiple React elements without adding extra nodes to the DOM
To provide a way to import external libraries into React projects
To handle errors and exceptions in React components
To manage routing in React applications

react.question: When should you use React's Context API?
options: When passing props through multiple levels of nested components becomes cumbersome
When handling asynchronous operations in React components
When styling components using CSS-in-JS libraries
When implementing routing in React applications

react.question: What is the purpose of React Router?
options: To manage state in React components
To handle API requests within React components
To provide navigation and routing functionalities in React applications
To define global styles for React components

react.question: What is the role of PropTypes in React?
options: To specify the data types of component props for type checking
To handle asynchronous operations in React components
To define the structure of a Redux store
To manage state transitions in React components

react.question: What is the purpose of React's useMemo hook?
options: To memoize expensive computations so that they can be cached and reused
To handle side effects in React components
To manage form data in React applications
To synchronize state across multiple components

react.question: What does the term 'unidirectional data flow' mean in the context of React?
options: Data flows only from child to parent components
Data flows only from parent to child components
Data flows in a circular pattern within components

react.question: What is the purpose of React's useCallback hook?
options: To memoize expensive computations so that they can be cached and reused
To handle side effects in React components
To manage form data in React applications
To memoize callback functions to prevent unnecessary re-renders
